title: Circuit Basics
subtitle: How Circuits Work & Safety Rules
overview: This session introduces students to electrical circuits, how electricity flows, and important safety rules while working with electrical components in real life.

images._note: Add photos from the actual session — wires, batteries, LEDs, switches, safety demonstrations, breadboard circuits.

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Photo of simple battery-LED circuit
gallery.caption: Simple electric circuit with battery and LED
Battery stores and supplies electrical energy
Wires carry electricity from one component to another
Switch controls ON and OFF flow of electricity
LED glows when current flows through circuit
Safety first while working with electricity

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Photo of students building circuit
gallery.caption: Students creating simple circuits on breadboard

keyTopics: What is a Circuit? — A closed path for electricity
Open Circuit — Broken path, no current flow
Closed Circuit — Complete path, current flows
Electrical Safety Rules in daily life
Real-life examples of circuits in home appliances

youtubeVideos.desc: How to build a basic LED circuit using battery and wires.

activity.title: Build a Simple LED Circuit
steps: Connect battery positive terminal to switch
Connect resistor to LED positive leg
Connect LED negative leg to battery negative terminal
Observe LED glowing in closed circuit
Disconnect one wire and observe open circuit

safetyRules: Never touch electric switches with wet hands
Do not insert metal objects into sockets
Always switch OFF power before repairing circuits
Keep water away from electrical devices
Use low-voltage batteries for school projects
Ask an adult before using high-voltage devices

options: A closed path for electricity to flow
quiz.explanation: A circuit is a complete path through which electricity flows.

quiz.q: Which component supplies energy in a simple circuit?
quiz.explanation: Battery provides electrical energy to the circuit.

quiz.q: What happens in an OPEN circuit?
options: The path is broken and current stops
quiz.explanation: In an open circuit, the electrical path is incomplete.

quiz.q: What is the job of a switch?
options: Control ON and OFF current flow
quiz.explanation: A switch opens or closes the circuit.

quiz.q: Which device converts electricity into light?
quiz.explanation: LED changes electrical energy into light energy.

quiz.q: Which is a real-life example of a circuit?
quiz.explanation: A torch contains a complete electrical circuit.

quiz.q: What should you NEVER do with electric switches?
quiz.explanation: Wet hands can cause electric shock.

quiz.q: What carries electricity in a circuit?
quiz.explanation: Wires provide a conducting path for current flow.

options: A direct dangerous connection causing excess current
quiz.explanation: Short circuits are dangerous and may cause fire or damage.

quiz.q: Why should damaged wires not be used?
options: They can cause electric shock or fire
quiz.explanation: Damaged wires are unsafe and dangerous.

quiz.q: Which circuit allows current to flow?
quiz.explanation: A closed circuit has a complete path for electricity.

quiz.q: What should students use for school circuit projects?
quiz.explanation: Low-voltage batteries are safer for beginners.

quiz.q: What happens when the switch is OFF?
quiz.explanation: OFF switch opens the circuit and stops current.
Power should always be switched OFF before repairs.
